flashcards.definition: Pollutant emitted directly into the atmosphere (CO, SO₂, NOₓ, PM, VOCs, lead).
Formed by chemical reactions of primary pollutants in the atmosphere (ozone, smog, acid deposition).
Six pollutants regulated by the Clean Air Act: CO, Pb (lead), NO₂, O₃ (ozone), PM, SO₂.
Brown haze formed when sunlight reacts with NOₓ and VOCs; worst in sunny cities with heavy traffic (LA).
Warm air layer traps cool air and pollutants near the ground, worsening air quality.
